topic: World Religions: Judaism / Islam / Buddhism / Hinduism
subject: religious-studies

items.q: What does karma mean in Hindu and Buddhist thought?
items.correct: The principle that actions have moral consequences shaping future experience or rebirth — not fate, but ethical cause and effect built into reality.
items.misconception: Karma is when bad things happen to bad people as punishment.
items.random_wrong: Karma is a god who decides what happens to you.
items.why: Students treat karma as cosmic justice meted out by a deity. In its philosophical origins it is impersonal moral causation — your own actions, not external judgement, shape your circumstances.

items.correct: The Islamic declaration of faith: 'There is no god but Allah, and Muhammad is the messenger of Allah' — affirming monotheism and Muhammad's prophethood.
items.misconception: The Shahada is the requirement to pray five times a day.
items.random_wrong: The Shahada is the Islamic pilgrimage to Mecca.
items.why: Students confuse the five pillars. Shahada is the declaration, Salah is prayer, Hajj is pilgrimage — each is a distinct pillar with a different function.

items.q: What is the difference between Sunni and Shia Islam?
items.correct: They split over who should lead the Muslim community after Muhammad: Sunnis accepted Abu Bakr and elected caliphs; Shias believed leadership should follow Muhammad's bloodline through Ali.
items.misconception: Sunnis and Shias believe in different gods.
items.random_wrong: Sunnis follow the Quran and Shias follow the Bible.
items.why: Both share the Quran, prophet and core beliefs. The original split was political — about succession — and theological differences developed later. Students inflate it into a different religion.

items.q: How many surahs are in the Qur'an?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 114. “66” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
Correct answer: Oneness of God. “Pilgrimage” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Over how many years was the Qur'an revealed?
items.why: The real figure is 23 years. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
